Notice Of Motion And Motion To Have Requests For Admission Propounded To Plaintiff Charles P. Schwartz, Iii Deemed Admitted, And For Monetary Sanctions Against Plaintiff And His Attorney, Donald Schwartz, In The Amount Of 2,930.00
Off Calendar Dec-10-2021 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:

APPEARANCES (VIA ZOOM VIDEOCONFERENCE):
Atty. THOMAS O'BRIEN, Esq. of De La Pena & Holiday LLP (Tel: 415-268-8000) for defendant Je & June, Inc.;
No appearances made in this matter.

HEARING RE: DEFENDANT JE & JUNE, INC.'s Motion To Have Requests For Admission Propounded To Plaintiff Charles P. Schwartz, III Deemed Admitted, And For Monetary Sanctions Against Plaintiff And His Attorney, Donald Schwartz, In The Amount Of $2,930.00.

Defendant stipulates to the matter being decided by the Judge Pro Tem. The Judge Pro Tem and counsel for defendant (both appearing remotely) authorize the clerk to sign the formal stipulation form on their behalf.

Matter not contested/argued. The Court adopts its tentative ruling.

Motion granted, no opposition filed. Requests for Admission Nos. 1-9 are deemed admitted by Plaintiff Charles Schwartz III. Objections are waived. Plaintiff Schwartz shall pay sanctions in the amount of $2,000 to De La Pena & Holiday LLP, within 30 days.

The prevailing party is required to prepare and submit a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the Court's ruling and must e-mail it to the Judge Pro Tem at aaron@minnisandsmallets.com.

Judge Pro Tem: Aaron Minnis; Clerk: Sean Kane; Not Reported. (302/JPT)

Notice Of Motion And Motion To Compel Responses To Rfas, And For Monetary Sanctions
Off Calendar Nov-15-2021 Denied
MINI MINUTES:

APPEARANCES (VIA ZOOM VIDEOCONFERENCE):
Atty. KEVIN LABARBERA, Esq. of de la Pena & Holiday LLP (Tel: 415-268-8000) for defendant Je & June, Inc.;
No other appearances are made in this matter.

HEARING RE: DEFENDANT JE & JUNE, INC.'s Motion To Compel Responses To Requests for Admissions, And For Monetary Sanctions.

Defendant stipulates to the Judge Pro Tem deciding the matter. The Judge Pro Tem and defense counsel (both appearing remotely) authorize the clerk to sign the formal stipulation form on their behalf.

Matter not contested/argued. The Court adopts its tentative ruling.

The motion is DENIED. The moving party asks this Court for an Order compelling responses to Requests for Admission pursuant to CCP section 2030.280. However, that section does not authorize such an Order. Rather, Section 2030.280 permits the propounding party to make a motion that the Requests for Admission be deemed admitted. Because the moving party did not seek that remedy in the Notice of Motion or anywhere in the legal arguments in it brief, the motion is defective and DENIED without prejudice.

Defense counsel to prepare and submit a proposed order in both .pdf and Word formats to the Judge Pro Tem by email sent to noah@ndllegal.com.

Judge Pro Tem: Noah Lebowitz; Clerk: Sean Kane; Not Reported. (302/JPT)
